Title: Proteomic and metabolic disturbances in lignin-modified Brachypodium distachyon

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem vascular bundles, sclerenchyma, interfascicular fibers, cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phe-derived entry route into phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate with ammonia release
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies entry precursor pool for monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: early control step; not established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highest transcript levels among monofunctional PALs in stems; abundant in mature stem internodes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth; RNAi knockdown; stable isotope feeding with 13C9-Phe or 13C9-Tyr in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; RT-qPCR; proteomics; lignin histochemistry; thioacidolysis; acetyl bromide lignin assay; targeted and untargeted metabolomics; stable isotope labeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAL2 knockdown modestly reduced lignin and slightly increased H-units; increased Phe, Tyr, shikimate, quinate, glutamine and glutamate; shifted labeling toward Tyr-derived incorporation into lignin intermediates/products
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL2 was selected as the major monofunctional PAL expressed in stems and successfully downregulated by RNAi. 2. BdPAL2i caused only modest effects on lignin composition, mainly a slight increase in H-units, indicating partial functional redundancy. 3. Knockdown increased aromatic amino acids and nitrogen-related metabolites, linking PAL2 activity to carbon/nitrogen partitioning. 4. Isotope labeling showed altered Phe/Tyr contribution to lignin intermediates and monolignols.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL2 protein was not clearly reduced at the protein level despite transcript reduction; Brachypodium contains multiple PAL family members, indicating redundancy and compensation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lodging, shorter internodes, reduced biomass relative to WT, but weaker lignin phenotype than other RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: proteomics, targeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics, untargeted GC-MS metabolomics; ProteomeXchange PXD026665; MassIVE MSV000087614

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem vascular bundles, sclerenchyma, interfascicular fibers, cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: dual Phe/Tyr entry route, preferential Tyr-derived route toward soluble hydroxycinnamates and S-lignin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate and tyrosine to p-coumarate; shows higher catalytic efficiency toward tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies direct precursor pool for monolignol biosynthesis and preferentially supports S-unit formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major upstream control point; highly abundant key control step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: most abundant lignin biosynthesis protein in mature stems; high stem expression in lignifying tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth; RNAi knockdown; 13C9-Phe and 13C9-Tyr feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; RT-qPCR; quantitative proteomics; lignin assays; LC-MS/MS targeted phenylpropanoid analysis; GC-MS untargeted metabolomics; stable isotope labeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PTAL1 knockdown strongly reduced lignin content and biomass, decreased S-unit proportion, reduced sinapaldehyde and 5-hydroxy-ferulate, increased cinnamate, and caused major shifts in amino acid and nitrogen-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTAL1 is the most abundant lignin biosynthetic protein in Brachypodium stems. 2. RNAi downregulation caused one of the strongest lignin and growth defects, with major reduction in lignin and altered monomer composition. 3. Data support preferential involvement of PTAL1 in S-unit biosynthesis via Tyr-derived p-coumarate/free acid route. 4. PTAL1 perturbation broadly altered amino acid and primary metabolism, consistent with a role in nitrogen recycling through ammonia release. 5. Isotope labeling showed reduced Tyr contribution and compensatory increased Phe flux into lignin intermediates when PTAL1 was knocked down.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PTAL1 and C3H/APX activities were discussed as potentially forming a high-flux cytosolic route via free phenolic acids; authors propose PTAL1 as central in coordinating carbon allocation to lignin and nitrogen recycling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: severe dwarfing/lodging, reduced biomass, broad metabolic shutdown when strongly suppressed
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: proteomics, targeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics, untargeted GC-MS metabolomics, stable isotope labeling; ProteomeXchange PXD026665; MassIVE MSV000087614

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Bradi2g53470; UniProt I1HKP5/I1HSV5 as reported inconsistently
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem cell walls of vascular and sclerenchyma tissues
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms central precursor for monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: early control step; not established as sole bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher transcript level than C4H2 in mature stem internodes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; RT-qPCR; proteomics; lignin staining and quantification; targeted and untargeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: C4H1 knockdown strongly reduced lignin and biomass, increased cinnamate, altered G and S proportions, and broadly disrupted nitrogen, glycerolipid and purine metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H1 is the major stem-expressed C4H family member targeted in this study. 2. RNAi downregulation caused severe lignin reduction, dwarfing, and cinnamate accumulation, validating its early role in monolignol biosynthesis. 3. BdC4H1i showed relatively higher coniferaldehyde and coniferyl alcohol and increased G-lignin proportion compared with WT. 4. Knockdown triggered broad proteomic/metabolic disturbance, including glutamine accumulation and repression of several lignin-related proteins.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper contains inconsistent UniProt listing for C4H1 between table and accession section.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: strong biomass penalty, lodging, extensive off-target pathway disturbance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: proteomics, targeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics, untargeted GC-MS metabolomics; ProteomeXchange PXD026665; MassIVE MSV000087614

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: lignin, with strong effects on guaiacyl lignin and Tyr-derived syringyl route
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor toward G-lignin and contributes to S-lignin through canonical shikimate shunt
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: single family member; highly expressed in mature stems
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth; RNAi knockdown; 13C9-Phe and 13C9-Tyr isotope feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; RT-qPCR; proteomics; targeted LC-MS/MS for phenylpropanoid intermediates; lignin quantification; isotope labeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: C3'H1 knockdown caused strong p-coumaroyl shikimate accumulation, reduced lignin content, increased H-units, reduced SA-related metabolites, and evidence for an alternative Tyr-to-sinapate/S-lignin route bypassing the canonical shikimate shunt
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3'H1 is the sole canonical C3'H enzyme in Brachypodium stems and a core shikimate-shunt enzyme. 2. Knockdown massively accumulated its substrate p-coumaroyl shikimate, validating in vivo function. 3. BdC3'H1i reduced lignin but did not abolish it, demonstrating that the canonical shikimate-shunt route is not essential for lignin formation in Brachypodium. 4. Metabolite and isotope data support stronger involvement of C3'H1 in G-lignin biosynthesis, while S-lignin can still arise via an alternative Tyr/sinapate route.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual caffeoyl shikimate in knockdown lines suggests remaining 3'-hydroxylase activity; authors infer alternative pathway for S-lignin in grasses.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lodging, reduced biomass (~25%), altered SA metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: proteomics, targeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics, untargeted GC-MS metabolomics, stable isotope labeling; ProteomeXchange PXD026665; MassIVE MSV000087614

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and biomass properties; relevant for biomass processing
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers p-coumaroyl group from p-coumaroyl-CoA to shikimate to form p-coumaroyl shikimate; can reverse from caffeoyl shikimate to caffeoyl-CoA inefficiently
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms canonical shikimate-shunt intermediate required mainly for G-lignin branch, but not essential for total lignin formation in Brachypodium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher expression than HCT2 in mature stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; RT-qPCR; proteomics; lignin quantification; targeted/untargeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HCT1 knockdown strongly reduced lignin content and altered monomer composition with increased H-units and S/G ratio, yet had little or no biomass penalty; increased SA and SA glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT1 is a core shikimate-shunt enzyme in Brachypodium stems. 2. RNAi downregulation strongly reduced lignin but did not reduce biomass, separating lignin reduction from growth penalty. 3. Data suggest HCT1 contributes more actively to G-lignin biosynthesis than to S-lignin in Brachypodium. 4. The persistence of lignification despite HCT1 knockdown supports that the canonical shikimate shunt is not essential in this grass. 5. HCT1i is highlighted as a promising target for improved saccharification and lignocellulosic biomass quality.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note reverse HCT reaction is inefficient in vitro and in vivo. HCT1i previously showed increased saccharification efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lodging and altered lignin composition but minimal biomass/yield penalty relative to other lines
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: proteomics, targeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics, untargeted GC-MS metabolomics; ProteomeXchange PXD026665; MassIVE MSV000087614
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising engineering target for low-lignin grass biomass with improved saccharification and limited growth penalty

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: lignin, especially free hydroxycinnamate-derived monolignol route
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes 3-hydroxylation of p-coumarate to caffeate in a soluble route distinct from the shikimate shunt
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies free hydroxycinnamic acid intermediates toward ferulate and monolignols, potentially favoring S-lignin route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: high-abundance alternative route enzyme; important control point suggested
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: among the most abundant lignin pathway proteins in mature stems; strong expression in roots and stems
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: measured in WT stem proteome; inferred under normal lignifying conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: quantitative proteomics; integrated pathway analysis; prior enzymology discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: identified as one of the most abundant lignin pathway proteins supporting a major soluble free-acid route from p-coumarate to caffeate in grasses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H/APX1 was identified as one of the most abundant lignin biosynthetic proteins in Brachypodium stems. 2. Its abundance, together with PTAL1 and COMT1, supports a major alternative lignin pathway via free phenolic acids. 3. The study proposes that this route may operate in parallel with the canonical shikimate shunt and contribute importantly to grass lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly manipulated in this paper; included because authors identify it as a core lignin enzyme and central to their metabolic model. Potential protein complex/co-purification with PTAL activity was discussed based on prior work.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: 4-O-methylates caffeate to ferulate and likely methylates 5-hydroxyferulate in monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies ferulate/sinapate pools for monolignol and especially S-unit biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not directly tested, but highly abundant key pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: second most abundant lignin pathway protein in mature stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: high abundance supports major role in free phenolic acid route to ferulate and S-lignin in grasses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COMT1 was one of the most abundant lignin biosynthesis proteins in mature Brachypodium stems. 2. Its abundance supports a prominent route from caffeate to ferulate/free acids rather than exclusive dependence on shikimate esters. 3. Together with PTAL1 and C3H/APX1, COMT1 underpins the proposed grass-specific alternative route to S-lignin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly silenced in this study; included as core protein highlighted by proteomics and central pathway model.
